etymology.description: semantic 車(車) (chē, "chariot") + phonetic component related to original forms of 螾 (yǐn, "earthworm"), 旬 (xún, "ten days"), 勻 (yún, "even"), 云 (yún, "say") -> formation of an organized group or army
etymology.interpretation: The character 軍(jūn) represents a military unit, consisting of a chariot 車(車) (chē, "chariot") as the semantic component, indicating mobility and equipment, paired with a phonetic component that historically is tied to concepts of organization and grouping, implying a unified military force.
etymology.simplification: The simplified form 军 reduces the character to essential strokes, maintaining the structure representing chariot imagery while resembling a fortification or group lining.

examples.chinese: 军队正在训练(軍隊正在訓練)。
他们决定加入军队(他們決定加入軍隊)。
examples.english: They decided to join the army.

examples.chinese: 古代的军队使用弓箭(古代的軍隊使用弓箭)，而不是现代的武器(而不是現代的武器)。
examples.pinyin: Gǔdài de jūnduì shǐyòng gōngjiàn, ér bùshì xiàndài de wǔqì.
examples.english: Ancient armies used bows and arrows, not modern weapons.
